text: Eliphaz: Can a Man Be of Use to God?

segments.text: Then Eliphaz the Temanite replied:
Can a man be of use to God? Can even a wise man benefit Him?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Does it delight the Almighty that you are righteous? Does He profit if your ways are blameless?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Is it for your reverence that He rebukes you and enters into judgment against you?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Is not your wickedness great? Are not your iniquities endless?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: For you needlessly demanded security from your brothers and deprived the naked of their clothing.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: You gave no water to the weary and withheld food from the famished,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: while the land belonged to a mighty man, and a man of honor lived on it.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: You sent widows away empty-handed, and the strength of the fatherless was crushed.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Therefore snares surround you, and sudden peril terrifies you;
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: it is so dark you cannot see, and a flood of water covers you.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Is not God as high as the heavens? Look at the highest stars, how lofty they are!
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Yet you say: ‘What does God know? Does He judge through thick darkness?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Thick clouds veil Him so He does not see usas He traverses the vault of heaven.’
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Will you stay on the ancient paththat wicked men have trod?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: They were snatched away before their time, and their foundations were swept away by a flood.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: They said to God, ‘Depart from us. What can the Almighty do to us?’
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: But it was He who filled their houses with good things; so I stay far from the counsel of the wicked.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: The righteous see it and are glad; the innocent mock them:
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: ‘Surely our foes are destroyed, and fire has consumed their excess.’
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Reconcile now and be at peace with Him; thereby good will come to you.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Receive instruction from His mouth, and lay up His words in your heart.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: If you return to the Almighty, you will be restored. If you remove injustice from your tents
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: and consign your gold to the dust and the gold of Ophir to the stones of the ravines,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: then the Almighty will be your gold and the finest silver for you.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Surely then you will delight in the Almighty and lift up your face to God.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: You will pray to Him, and He will hear you, and you will fulfill your vows.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Your decisions will be carried out, and light will shine on your ways.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: When men are brought low and you say, ‘Lift them up!’then He will save the lowly.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: He will deliver even one who is not innocent, rescuing him through the cleanness of your hands.
segments.speaker: Unknown
